questions.question: What is the primary function of a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) in image processing?
options: A) To classify images into different categories
B) To detect edges and lines in images
C) To recognize objects in images
D) To compress images
questions.explanation: The primary function of a CNN is to recognize objects in images by learning spatial hierarchies of features.

questions.question: What is the key difference between a 1D and 2D Convolutional Neural Network?
options: A) 1D CNN uses convolution operation on spatial dimensions, while 2D CNN uses it on spatial dimensions
B) 1D CNN uses convolution operation on spatial dimensions, while 2D CNN uses it on temporal dimensions
C) 1D CNN uses convolution operation on temporal dimensions, while 2D CNN uses it on spatial dimensions
D) 1D CNN uses convolution operation on temporal dimensions, while 2D CNN uses it on spatial dimensions
questions.explanation: The key difference between a 1D and 2D CNN is that 1D CNN uses convolution operation on temporal dimensions, while 2D CNN uses it on spatial dimensions.

questions.question: What is the purpose of padding in a Convolutional Neural Network?
options: A) To reduce the number of parameters in the network
B) To increase the receptive field of the network
C) To prevent vanishing gradients
D) To improve the accuracy of the network
questions.explanation: Padding is used to increase the receptive field of the network, allowing it to capture features at different scales.

questions.question: What is the effect of increasing the number of filters in a Convolutional Neural Network?
options: A) It reduces the capacity of the network
B) It increases the capacity of the network
C) It reduces the number of parameters in the network
D) It increases the number of parameters in the network
questions.explanation: Increasing the number of filters in a CNN increases the capacity of the network, allowing it to learn more complex features.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Max Pooling in a Convolutional Neural Network?
options: A) To reduce the dimensionality of the feature maps
B) To increase the number of parameters in the network
C) To improve the accuracy of the network
D) To reduce the number of features in the network
questions.explanation: Max Pooling is used to reduce the dimensionality of the feature maps, reducing the computational requirements of the network.
